default_creds.context: No default admin account ships. The first self-registered user becomes admin (docs: 'the first user created becomes the admin'). Risk is the OPEN registration gate plus first-user-wins admin assignment, not a hardcoded credential.

.env.example ships this exact JWT signing secret (and JWT_REFRESH_SECRET eaa5191f..., CREDS_KEY f34be427..., CREDS_IV e2341419...). Operators who copy .env.example and never rotate run forgeable session tokens and repo-decryptable stored upstream API keys.

install_tell: git clone https://github.com/danny-avila/LibreChat.git && cd LibreChat && cp .env.example .env && docker compose up -d   # then browse http://localhost:3080
misconfig_patterns: ALLOW_REGISTRATION=true left at default on an internet-exposed port -> anyone self-registers; first registrant becomes admin if operator hasn't yet
JWT_SECRET/JWT_REFRESH_SECRET/CREDS_KEY/CREDS_IV left at the published .env.example values -> forgeable sessions + decryptable stored provider API keys
mongodb --noauth exposed to a shared/over-permissive network -> direct datastore read
rag_api / vectordb / meilisearch ports published to the host instead of kept on the internal compose network
MCP server / Actions / custom-endpoint features enabled without actions.allowedDomains or private-IP filtering -> SSRF + RCE chain
Outdated build (<= 0.8.3) exposed -> MCP env-var-injection root RCE reachable by any registered user

passive: http.html:"LibreChat - An open source chat application with support for multiple AI models"

active_probe.false_positive_check: The HTML title "LibreChat" alone is weak (could collide with forks, mirrors, or pages that merely mention LibreChat). Confirm by GET /api/config (no auth needed) and require the JSON to contain the LibreChat-unique field cluster together: appTitle + registrationEnabled + emailLoginEnabled + socialLogins + openidLoginEnabled + buildInfo. That object shape is vendor-unique. A generic chat app or a reverse-proxy error page will not return this exact JSON schema. Also distinguish a true LibreChat backend from a static clone of the SPA: the clone serves index.html but /api/config returns 404/HTML, not the JSON object. registrationEnabled value (true/false) is the per-deployment posture read, not the fingerprint.

deployment_tells: GET /api/config returns the LibreChat JSON field cluster (appTitle/registrationEnabled/buildInfo) with no auth
Companion containers from registry.librechat.ai: librechat-dev, librechat-rag-api-dev-lite
Sidecar services: meilisearch (getmeili/meilisearch, port 7700), pgvector vectordb (pgvector/pgvector, 5432), mongo:8.0 with --noauth (27017), rag_api on 8000
buildInfo.commit / buildInfo.branch in /api/config reveals the running build for CVE scoping
Fronting reverse proxy commonly maps 80/443 -> 3080 (nginx/traefik per docs deploy guides)
pivot_paths: GET /api/config (unauth) -> read appTitle, registrationEnabled, socialLogins, openid/saml/ldap flags, buildInfo (version for CVE scoping), and modelSpecs system prompts / internal endpoint names
GET /api/banner and /api/endpoints (read provider/endpoint config exposed pre-auth where applicable)
Read registrationEnabled:true to confirm the open-registration posture WITHOUT registering (names are the finding; do not create an account on a third-party host)
Favicon hash + buildInfo to cluster instances by operator/version across the harvested IP set
Cross-reference buildInfo.branch/commit against the GHSA list to scope which instances are pre-fix for the MCP-RCE / SSRF chain
vulnerabilities: CVE-2026-32625 / GHSA-cxhj-j78r-p88f (CVSS 9.8 Critical) — MCP stdio environment-variable injection -> authenticated RCE as root in the Docker container; any registerable user can reach it; affects <= 0.8.3
GHSA-4pcc-j6m6-wcwx (Critical) — Server secrets exfiltration via MCP server URL injection
CVE-2025-69222 / GHSA-rgjq-4q58-m3q8 (High SSRF) — Actions feature lacks domain restriction in default config; fixed 0.8.2-rc2 via actions.allowedDomains; affects 0.8.1-rc2
GHSA-gc9r-88c3-7qhq (High SSRF) — user-provided custom endpoint baseURL has no private-IP validation -> cloud metadata / internal reach
GHSA-gvpj-vm2f-2m23 (High) — missing resource-parameter validation in MCP OAuth flow
CVE-2026-44653 — agent permission bypass; CVE-2026-31942 — IDOR (access-control cluster with CVE-2026-32625)
GHSA-5ccx-4r3h-9qc7 — insufficient access control on agent permission queries
GHSA-6vqg-rgpm-qvf9 (Moderate) — shared MCP server view leaks decrypted admin secrets
GHSA-qrm5-r67f-6692 (Moderate) — arbitrary file write via execute_code artifact filename traversal

GHSA-g445-9wq6-jf3v — missing rate limiting (incomplete fix for CVE-2025-7105); GHSA-8892-xj8q-59xc — IDOR message deletion (incomplete fix for CVE-2024-41703); GHSA-52f6-fqwv-jccf — Multer missing file-size limits (incomplete fix for CVE-2024-11171)
CVE-2024-11173 — open registration enables unauthenticated attacker to create an account (the registration-gate exposure underpinning the authenticated-RCE chain)
GHSA-3phr-62qf-cxf3 (Moderate) — stored XSS via unescaped image alt text in markdown artifact preview
Info disclosure (issue #12688) — /api/config exposes modelSpecs/system-prompts to unauthenticated users (not yet a CVE)
auth_evidence: Default `.env.example` (the file the docs' quickstart copies verbatim: `cp .env.example .env`) ships `ALLOW_EMAIL_LOGIN=true` and `ALLOW_REGISTRATION=true` with `HOST=localhost PORT=3080`. The official docker quickstart docs state: "you'll see the login page where you can register your account - the first user created becomes the admin." So authentication IS forced at the UI/chat layer (no anonymous chat; login required), which makes the base posture configurable-default-on. The load-bearing caveat: registration is OPEN by default and there is NO admin-approval gate and NO privileged first-run setup token. On a fresh internet-exposed instance the FIRST self-registered user becomes admin; if the operator has not yet registered, an attacker who reaches the port first claims admin. `.env.example` also ships SHARED, PUBLISHED-IN-REPO default JWT_SECRET / JWT_REFRESH_SECRET / CREDS_KEY / CREDS_IV values that many operators never rotate (forgeable sessions / decryptable stored API keys). docker-compose.yml runs mongodb with `mongod --noauth`, so the backing datastore is unauthenticated by container default (only network-isolated, not access-controlled).
sensitive_assets: Upstream LLM provider API keys (OpenAI/Anthropic/Azure/Google/etc.) stored encrypted with CREDS_KEY/CREDS_IV — repo-default key values are decryptable if operator never rotated .env.example
Unauthenticated /api/config leaks modelSpecs = full system prompts for every configured model, internal/custom endpoint names, and infrastructure config (GitHub issue #12688)
Ingested RAG documents and uploaded files (rag_api + pgvector vectordb) — business documents, potential PII
Conversation history and saved prompts/presets for all users once an account is obtained
Agent / MCP server definitions (decrypted admin secrets leak via shared MCP view, GHSA-6vqg-rgpm-qvf9; secrets exfil via MCP URL injection, GHSA-4pcc-j6m6-wcwx)
Mongodb runs --noauth — full conversation/user store readable to anyone with container/network reach
Forgeable user/admin sessions if default JWT_SECRET unrotated
sources: [CANDIDATE - NuClide Cat-34 rag-app-builders Stage -1 OSINT 2026-06-12; software-default characterization, no live host probed yet]
